serving.natural_language_portion: One standard ala carte serving of Veggie Delite is approximately 150 g, consisting of a mixed vegetable assembly typically including lettuce, tomato, cucumber, green pepper, and onion.

nutrient_source.title: Veggie Delite Ala Carte — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy (55 kcal/100g), protein (10.7 g/100g), fat (0.0 g/100g), and carbohydrate (2.36 g/100g) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record for 'Veggie Delite Ala Carte' (cr_id G00010429). The protein value of 10.7 g/100g is unusually high for a vegetable-only dish and is preserved as reported; verification with the vendor is recommended. Fat is reported as 0.0 g/100g by HPB; this is treated as confirmed zero per the approved source. Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, sugar, saturated fat) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central mixed vegetable salad analogues (USDA SR Legacy #11979 and related entries) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; mixed vegetable salad analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Veggie Delite Ala Carte' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 15 is estimated by analogy to raw mixed vegetable salads (predominantly non-starchy leafy vegetables, tomato, cucumber, pepper, onion) which consistently yield GI values in the range 10–20 in peer-reviewed GI tables. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (15 × 2.36 × 1.5) / 100 ≈ 0.5, classified as very low. Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Low estimated sodium (~150 mg/100g) with favourable potassium (~250 mg/100g) ratio
hypertension.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed without high-sodium dressings or sauces; the estimated sodium content of ~150 mg/100g and potassium content of ~250 mg/100g support a favourable Na:K ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles, but actual sodium may vary significantly if pre-seasoned dressings are applied.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100g) and estimated low GI (~15) with negligible glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: The Veggie Delite Ala Carte is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content (2.36 g/100g), estimated low glycaemic index (~15), and negligible glycaemic load per serving (~0.5); patients should avoid high-sugar dressings and note that the reported protein value of 10.7 g/100g is atypically high for a vegetable-only dish and should be verified with the vendor before use in precise meal planning.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero reported fat and saturated fat with high vegetable antioxidant content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is cardioprotective in its base form, with zero reported total fat and saturated fat per HPB data, and a vegetable composition rich in dietary fibre, antioxidants (vitamin C, carotenoids), and potassium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid adding high-fat or high-sodium dressings that would negate these benefits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory vegetable profile; nightshade vegetables present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Fresh vegetables in this dish provide antioxidants and dietary fibre supporting anti-inflammatory pathways; however, individuals with documented nightshade sensitivity should be aware that tomato, bell pepper, and onion are present, and solanine-containing nightshades may aggravate symptoms in sensitive RA patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veggie Delite Ala Carte is a Singapore-served Western-style fresh vegetable salad dish, typically composed of a mix of raw leafy greens (lettuce), tomato, cucumber, green bell pepper, onion, and olives, assembled without cooking. It is sold as an ala carte item, meaning it is ordered independently rather than as part of a set meal. The dish is minimally processed, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein (an unusually high figure for a vegetable-only dish that warrants vendor verification), 0 g fat, and 2.36 g carbohydrate. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 15 (low), making it an excellent choice for glycaemic control. The dish is rich in dietary fibre, antioxidants including vitamin C and carotenoids, and potassium, while being very low in sodium when served without dressings. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. Nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper) are present, which may be relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or nightshade sensitivity. The dish is best consumed without high-sugar or high-sodium dressings to preserve its nutritional benefits.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Veggie Delite ala carte?
Is Veggie Delite suitable for diabetics?
What vegetables are in Veggie Delite?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, fat, carbohydrate) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with high confidence. However, the protein value of 10.7 g/100g is atypically high for a vegetable-only dish and introduces uncertainty. Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, sugar, saturated fat, potassium) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central mixed vegetable salad analogues rather than directly measured for this specific dish. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010429); this is a confirmed zero from an approved source. saturated_fat_g and trans_fat_g are also confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero consistent with a plant-only dish. 2. The protein value of 10.7 g/100g is preserved as reported by HPB but is flagged as unusually high for a vegetable-only dish; vendor verification is strongly recommended before clinical use. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific dish. 4. calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this dish and not available with sufficient specificity from USDA analogues to assign with confidence. 5. sugar_g (1.2 g/100g), dietary_fibre_g (1.5 g/100g), sodium_mg (150 mg/100g), potassium_mg (250 mg/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central mixed vegetable salad analogues (SR Legacy entries for raw mixed vegetables) and are approximate. 6. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on non-starchy raw mixed vegetable salads from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 7. Nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper) are present; relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or nightshade sensitivity.
